steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence states a permanent scientific fact: "a xenon atom ______ 54 protons." The subject "a xenon atom" is singular. The verb must be present-tense simple to express a timeless, ongoing truth.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — had: past tense — implies the xenon atom no longer has 54 protons, which contradicts a permanent fact. ✗
  • Choice B — has had: present perfect — implies an action completed up to now rather than a standing fact. ✗
  • Choice C — has: present-simple, singular — correctly states a timeless fact about a xenon atom. ✓
  • Choice D — is having: present progressive — implies a temporary, ongoing action rather than a permanent property. ✗
C is correct.
